content: how far is the earth from its sun?

The average distance from the Earth to the Sun is approximately 93 million miles (149.6 million kilometers).

Classification: astronomy

No, mammals do not lay eggs. Mammals give birth to live young, a characteristic that distinguishes them from birds and reptiles, which are egg-laying animals.

Classification: biology

The platypus is a unique example of an animal that lays eggs, but also produces milk to feed its young like other mammals. This characteristic makes it one of the few venomous mammals and one of the most unusual animals in the world.

Classification: biology
